name: revenue
label: Revenue
caveats: Order-grain booked revenue: SUM(orders.total_amount) over orders not cancelled. Cancelled orders never delivered, so they are excluded by default. Refunded orders WERE fulfilled then reversed — include them in gross booked revenue; exclude them only when net-of-returns is explicitly requested.
wrong_usage_examples: SUM(order_items.line_total) — line-item grain; on this data it diverges ~4.3x from order totals (a different basis, not the same revenue). Use orders.total_amount for order/total revenue; reserve line_total for product/category-grain revenue where order totals cannot be apportioned.
SUM(total_amount) joined to order_items without de-duplicating to one row per order — the join fans out and multiplies revenue by the line count.

name: aov
label: Average Order Value
caveats: AVG(orders.total_amount) at order grain. NEVER AVG(order_items.line_total) — that is per-line, not per-order, and undercounts AOV by the average lines-per-order. Cancelled orders excluded by default; on this data excluding them barely moves AOV (cancelled orders are average-sized).
wrong_usage_examples: AVG(line_total) sold as AOV — that is average LINE value, not average ORDER value (the $467-vs-$1108 grain confusion).

name: revenue
label: Revenue
caveats: beautycommerce gross line revenue at order_items grain. final_price_usd is the PER-UNIT price AFTER discount, so revenue = SUM(final_price_usd * quantity) — NEVER SUM(final_price_usd) alone (that drops quantity and undercounts ~50%; avg quantity is ~2, so $252M instead of the correct $503M). Exclude non-real orders by joining analytics.orders on order_id and filtering order_status NOT IN ('cancelled','test','fraud'). For NET-of-returns revenue use analytics.invoices.revenue_net instead (~2.4% lower).
wrong_usage_examples: SUM(final_price_usd) without * quantity — final_price_usd is per-unit, so this undercounts revenue by the average quantity per line (~2x on this data: $252M vs the correct $503M).
SUM(orders.total_amount) — beautycommerce has no total_amount column; that is the samples-connection formula.

name: aov
label: Average Order Value
caveats: beautycommerce AOV = gross line revenue / distinct orders at order_items grain: SUM(final_price_usd * quantity) / COUNT(DISTINCT order_id). final_price_usd is per-unit, so the quantity multiplier is REQUIRED — AVG(final_price_usd) or a per-order SUM(final_price_usd) without quantity undercounts AOV ~2x (the $78 vs the correct ~$156 bug). Exclude cancelled/test/fraud orders.
wrong_usage_examples: AVG(final_price_usd) or a per-order SUM(final_price_usd) sold as AOV — drops the quantity multiplier (per-unit price), undercounting AOV ~2x ($78 vs the correct ~$156).
